analysis_summary: The proposed method produced 10 finding(s) from 12 analysis obligation(s).

findings.summary: External debug_req_i can force debug entry and PC redirection with only AMO/commit-state gating; no authorization/lifecycle guard is observed.
findings.vulnerability_category: enter debug / redirect PC / debug_mode_q/debug_mode_o, dpc_q, set_debug_pc_o, frontend PC control / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: external debug requester via top-level debug_req_i Operation: enter debug / redirect PC Object: debug_mode_q/debug_mode_o, dpc_q, set_debug_pc_o, frontend PC control Expected guard: Debug entry and PC redirection should be mediated by a debug authorization/lifecycle/platform policy, not only by request assertion and pipeline state. Observed behavior: Top-level debug_req is debug_req_i & ~amo_valid_commit. In csr_regfile, while !debug_mode_q, debug_req_i && commit_instr_i[0].valid saves pc_i to dpc_d, sets debug_mode_d=1, asserts set_debug_pc_o=1, and records CauseRequest. No authorization/lifecycle guard was observed. Path: src/ariane.sv:231-233 debug_req_i & ~amo_valid_commit -> src/ariane.sv:519 csr_regfile.debug_req_i -> src/csr_regfile.sv:640-650 debug entry -> src/ariane.sv:257 frontend.set_debug_pc_i; debug mode selects dm::ExceptionAddress at csr_regfile.sv:894-897. Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Requester able to assert debug_req_i can force privileged debug mode and debug PC redirect without an observed RTL authorization guard.

findings.summary: Machine CSR update/read decode is present, but privilege enforcement is separated from write latching and includes suspicious exceptions/privilege mapping; protected machine CSRs may be accessible from lower privilege in the observed RTL.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write CSR / machine CSRs mstatus, mtvec, medeleg, mideleg, mie, mip, mscratch, mepc, mcause, mtval, mcycle/minstret and machine trap/interrupt policy / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software CSR instruction path Operation: read/write CSR Object: machine CSRs mstatus, mtvec, medeleg, mideleg, mie, mip, mscratch, mepc, mcause, mtval, mcycle/minstret and machine trap/interrupt policy Expected guard: Machine CSR reads/writes should be blocked or trap when current privilege is below M-mode; writes should honor read-only CSR encoding. Observed behavior: CSR privilege exception logic reportedly checks csr_addr.csr_decode.priv_lvl versus priv_lvl_o, but write cases directly update machine CSR d-state when csr_we is asserted. Sequential block latches *_d to *_q without visible gating by csr_exception_o. The privilege check has an exception for CSR_MEPC and priv_lvl_o is forced to M when debug_mode_q or umode_i is asserted. Path: csr_op_i/csr_addr_i/csr_wdata_i -> csr_op_logic csr_we/csr_read at csr_regfile.sv:737-779 -> machine CSR update cases around csr_regfile.sv:437-495 -> sequential latching around csr_regfile.sv:964-1038; exception_ctrl privilege checks around csr_regfile.sv:852-857. Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Lower-privilege CSR instructions may update/read machine-protected CSRs before or independent of exception handling; MEPC appears explicitly exempted from privilege check.

findings.summary: Supervisor CSR update/read paths lack local U-mode/TVM enforcement in observed RTL; umode_i also appears to force current privilege output to M.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write/configure / supervisor trap and translation state including SATP, SSTATUS fields, STVEC, SEPC, SIE/SIP / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software CSR instruction path from U/S execution Operation: read/write/configure Object: supervisor trap and translation state including SATP, SSTATUS fields, STVEC, SEPC, SIE/SIP Expected guard: Supervisor CSRs should be inaccessible from U-mode; S-mode writes to sensitive controls should be subject to privilege and machine control bits such as TVM/TSR/TW where applicable. Observed behavior: S-mode CSR read/write cases expose and update SSTATUS/SIE/SIP/STVEC/SSCRATCH/SEPC/SCAUSE/STVAL/SATP. General privilege exception logic exists, but priv_lvl_o is forced to M when umode_i is asserted. SATP write updates satp_d for supported modes with no observed TVM check in csr_update; TVM/TW/TSR are only observed as outputs to id_stage. Path: csr_addr_i -> supervisor read mux csr_regfile.sv:197-209; write cases csr_regfile.sv:397-435 -> outputs satp_ppn_o/asid_o/sum_o/mxr_o/tvm_o/tw_o/tsr_o at csr_regfile.sv:943-954 -> ariane/id/ex connections. Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: U-mode may be treated as M-mode for CSR checks, and S-mode may alter translation/trap controls without visible machine restriction mediation.

findings.summary: CSR read mux exposes protected values before/independent of privilege exception handling and only treats nonexistent CSRs as read_access_exception in the visible read process.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/expose / csr_rdata_o for protected machine/supervisor/debug/performance state / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software CSR read path Operation: read/expose Object: csr_rdata_o for protected machine/supervisor/debug/performance state Expected guard: Read access should enforce CSR privilege and counter-enable policy; sensitive trap scratch/PC or translation configuration should not be exposed to lower privilege. Observed behavior: Read process comment states read_access_exception can only occur when attempting to read a CSR which does not exist. The read mux returns supervisor state, machine state, cache controls, counters, and perf_data_i. Privilege exceptions are generated later/separately in exception_ctrl, while csr_rdata_o is assigned csr_rdata with only MIP/SIP overlay. Debug CSR accesses outside debug are exceptioned separately. Path: csr_addr_i -> csr_read_process read case -> csr_rdata -> csr_rdata_o output assignment at csr_regfile.sv:920-935; exception_ctrl later sets illegal instruction for privilege/read exceptions. Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Protected machine/supervisor trap and policy registers, counters, and perf state may be driven on csr_rdata_o even when an illegal instruction exception is raised, risking exposure if downstream does not suppress writeback.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Downstream commit/writeback suppression for csr_rdata_o on csr_exception_o was not included in observations.

findings.summary: Translation policy CSRs can be updated in CSR write cases without observed privilege/TVM guard or automatic TLB flush on SATP/status policy change.
findings.vulnerability_category: configure memory access policy / SATP mode/PPN/ASID, SUM, MXR, MPRV, translation enable outputs / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software CSR write path Operation: configure memory access policy Object: SATP mode/PPN/ASID, SUM, MXR, MPRV, translation enable outputs Expected guard: Translation and memory access policy updates must require sufficient privilege, and changes to SATP/status bits should be synchronized with required flush/TLB invalidation behavior or architectural mediation. Observed behavior: SSTATUS write modifies mstatus fields and asserts flush_o. SATP write masks ASID and updates satp_d if mode is supported, with no observed TVM privilege guard and no explicit flush_o/TLB flush in that SATP case. Outputs directly drive MMU controls. ariane connects flush_tlb_ctrl_ex from controller sfence/fence paths, not observed from SATP writes. Path: CSR write path -> SSTATUS/SATP/MSTATUS update cases -> satp_q/mstatus_q -> satp_ppn_o/asid_o/sum_o/mxr_o/en_translation_o/en_ld_st_translation_o -> ex_stage memory management inputs; controller flush_tlb_o via sfence_vma_i. Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Lower or restricted modes may alter virtual memory permission policy; SATP changes may leave stale TLB/translation state absent explicit sfence/flush mediation.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: No full controller/id_stage evidence showing TVM/SFENCE handling was provided.

findings.summary: xRET operations are decoded into state-changing return controls without observed privilege/debug-state guard in csr_op_logic; DRET can select dpc_q even outside debug mode unless separately blocked downstream.
findings.vulnerability_category: mode transition / return / privilege mode, debug mode, exception PC and return control / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software instruction stream / debug return path Operation: mode transition / return Object: privilege mode, debug mode, exception PC and return control Expected guard: MRET only in M-mode, SRET only in S-mode when not forbidden by TSR, DRET only in debug mode. Observed behavior: csr_op_logic sets sret/mret/dret purely from csr_op_i and only clears them on ex_i.valid. priv_output selects sepc_q for sret and dpc_q for dret. Observations mention DRET clears debug_mode_q in update logic, but no privilege/debug-state guard for xRET decode was observed in the provided snippets. TVM/TW/TSR are output to id_stage but TSR check is not visible in csr_regfile return update path. Path: csr_op_i -> csr_op_logic SRET/MRET/DRET at csr_regfile.sv:745-767 -> mret/sret/dret state updates and eret_o/epc_o/trap_vector_base_o -> frontend/controller PC redirection. Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Illegal lower-privilege or non-debug return instructions may change privilege/debug state or redirect PC if not blocked elsewhere.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Instruction decode/id_stage may raise illegal instruction for xRET privilege violations, but that evidence was not provided.

findings.summary: PMP CSR addresses are defined but no PMP state, CSR read/write cases, lock-bit behavior, or memory permission use sites are observed in the visible scope.
findings.vulnerability_category: configure/change access policy / PMP configuration/address registers and memory access-control policy / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software CSR write path Operation: configure/change access policy Object: PMP configuration/address registers and memory access-control policy Expected guard: PMP CSRs should be M-mode writable only and locked entries prevent writes until reset; LSU/fetch should consume PMP permissions consistently. Observed behavior: include/riscv_pkg.sv defines CSR_PMPCFG0 and CSR_PMPADDR0. csr_regfile read/write excerpts do not show PMP cases; default read/update access exception appears to handle unsupported addresses. No PMP q/d registers or LSU/fetch PMP permission connections appear in fact layer. Path: CSR_PMPCFG0/CSR_PMPADDR0 definitions -> absent csr_regfile decode/state -> default exceptions if accessed; no observed memory permission outputs/use sites. Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: If PMP is expected by platform/security model, absence of implemented PMP enforcement means memory access control policy cannot be configured/enforced. If intentionally unsupported, CSR accesses appear trapped/ignored via default exception, but no PMP protection exists.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Full source outside snippets could implement PMP elsewhere; provided fact layer did not show it.

findings.summary: Counter and performance CSR reads/writes are exposed without observed counter-enable mediation; mcounteren/scounteren are read as zero/not implemented.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write/expose performance state / cycle/instret/performance counters and perf counter external interface / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software CSR and perf counter path Operation: read/write/expose performance state Object: cycle/instret/performance counters and perf counter external interface Expected guard: Performance counter reads/writes should respect privilege/counter-enable policy and machine-only counter CSRs should not be writable/readable by lower modes unless delegated/enabled. Observed behavior: Read mux returns CSR_CYCLE/CSR_INSTRET and performance counters from perf_data_i; machine MCYCLE/MINSTRET read cycle_q/instret_q. SCOUNTEREN/MCOUNTEREN read as 0/not implemented. perf_addr_o is driven by csr_addr and performance write path can assert perf_we_o for perf CSR writes. No counter-enable guard was observed. Path: csr_addr_i -> perf_addr_o and read mux -> perf_data_i/cycle_q/instret_q -> csr_rdata_o; write cases around csr_regfile.sv:508-509 assert perf_data_o/perf_we_o; ariane connects to perf_counters at ariane.sv:530-549. Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Lower privilege may read timing/performance side-channel state or modify performance counter interface without intended counter-enable permission checks.

findings.summary: Custom cache-control CSRs at 0x700/0x701 are readable/writable and drive cache enable outputs without observed privilege guard; icache disables in debug but dcache does not.
findings.vulnerability_category: configure cache controls / CSR_ICACHE/CSR_DCACHE and icache_en_o/dcache_en_o / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software CSR write path Operation: configure cache controls Object: CSR_ICACHE/CSR_DCACHE and icache_en_o/dcache_en_o Expected guard: Cache control CSRs should require machine privilege or platform-authorized mode and should not be writable by lower privilege to alter system behavior or bypass maintenance expectations. Observed behavior: CSR_DCACHE and CSR_ICACHE are decoded in read mux and machine/custom write cases. Outputs are icache_q[0] & ~debug_mode_q and dcache_q[0]. No local privilege guard for custom 0x700/0x701 writes is visible, and read/write accessibility depends on the general CSR privilege decode whose behavior is questionable in other findings. Path: CSR_ICACHE/CSR_DCACHE addresses -> icache_q/dcache_q read/write -> icache_en_o/dcache_en_o -> cache subsystem icache_en_i/dcache paths; controller flush signals separate. Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Lower privilege may alter cache enable state or observe cache control state, potentially affecting system behavior/timing or maintenance invariants.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Reset defaults and exact write cases for CSR_ICACHE/CSR_DCACHE were only partially observed.

findings.summary: Reset evidence confirms some safe defaults are intended, but full reset assignments for all protected CSR/debug/cache/translation state are truncated; cannot close the obligation.
findings.vulnerability_category: reset/initialize / security-relevant CSR state after reset / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: reset/lifecycle logic Operation: reset/initialize Object: security-relevant CSR state after reset Expected guard: Reset defaults should place protected state in safe privilege/permission configuration: debug inactive, translation/cache controls intentional, trap vectors initialized, no stale debug/scratch PC exposure. Observed behavior: csr_regfile has asynchronous active-low reset. Tool observations state priv_lvl_q resets to M and debug_mode_q resets to 0; mtvec_rst_load_q first-cycle boot_addr_i workaround exists. Many protected q registers are latched in sequential block, but complete reset assignment list is truncated. Outputs show translation depends on satp_q.mode and priv_lvl_o, caches depend on icache_q/dcache_q, and debug PC redirection depends on debug_mode_q/set_debug_pc_o. Path: rst_ni -> csr_regfile sequential block around csr_regfile.sv:964-1043; mtvec_rst_load_q workaround around csr_regfile.sv:299-305; outputs at csr_regfile.sv:943-961; top-level debug_req during reset release via ariane.sv:231-233. Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: If incomplete/unsafe reset defaults exist, stale debug PC/scratch, unintended translation/cache enablement, or unsafe trap vectors could occur; provided evidence insufficient to confirm or refute.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Complete reset branch assignments for dpc_q, dscratch*, mtvec_q, mstatus/satp, icache_q/dcache_q, counters, and mtvec_rst_load_q were not provided.

global_uncertainty: No closure-level missing evidence was recorded beyond the visible source scope limitations.
